task_completion.description: Open the human insulin GenBank file in UGENE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE graphical interface is open with 'human_insulin_gene.gb' loaded and visible in the project or sequence view.

task_completion.description: Locate and extract the exact coding sequence (CDS).
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing the CDS annotation specifically selected, highlighted, or extracted into a new sequence view.

task_completion.description: Generate the reverse complement of the CDS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing the reverse complement operation being performed or the resulting antisense sequence displayed.

task_completion.description: Export both the sense and antisense sequences as FASTA files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or UGENE export dialog confirming the creation of 'insulin_cds_sense.fasta' and 'insulin_cds_antisense.fasta' in the '~/UGENE_Data/results/antisense_probe/' directory.

task_completion.description: Record the exact CDS length and GC content in the probe design report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing 'probe_design_report.txt' containing the exact length '333' (or '333 bp'/'333 nt') and a GC content of approximately '64.6%'.

task_completion.description: Record the first 30 nucleotides of both strands and a suitability note in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor showing 'probe_design_report.txt' with two 30-nucleotide sequences and a note concluding that the GC content is NOT suitable (because 64.6% is outside the ideal 40-60% range).

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE GUI to analyze the sequence.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the UGENE graphical interface being used to open the file and analyze the sequence. The agent should not bypass UGENE by using terminal commands (e.g., grep, awk) or Python scripts to parse the GenBank file.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the report rather than using pre-computed answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must be seen interacting with a text editor to write the report or copying values from UGENE. It should not instantly create the final report file using a single terminal command with pre-computed values.

integrity.description: Files were saved in the correct requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output files must be explicitly saved to '~/UGENE_Data/results/antisense_probe/', not left in default UGENE directories, the home directory, or the Desktop.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exact CDS length for the provided human insulin gene is 333 nucleotides. The GC content is approximately 64.6%. Because 64.6% is outside the ideal range of 40-60%, the report should correctly note that it is NOT suitable for hybridization. The expected results directory is /home/ga/UGENE_Data/results/antisense_probe.
